channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: MAGAvenue: Lawmakers prep legislation to name several heartland highways after Trump
item.description: Missouri lawmakers are working to designate several stretches of Show-Me State highways after President-elect Trump, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court to weigh state moves to cut off Medicaid funding to Planned Parenthood
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court said Wednesday it will consider South Carolina's move to cut off Medicaid funding to Planned Parenthood, the latest abortion-related case since the justices overturned Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Andy Beshear says Democrats should set partisanship aside, work to meet people's needs
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear said Wednesday that Democrats should be thinking less about partisan politics and more about meeting people's everyday needs as they chart a comeback strategy after last month's crushing election losses.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rand Paul blocks bill to authorize local, state authorities to track drones
item.description: Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.) on Wednesday blocked an effort by Senate Democrats to pass legislation to authorize and provide resources to state and local authorities to track mysterious drones that have been sighted over New Jersey, New York and other states.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Paxton files motion to block auction of border wall materials
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton filed a motion Tuesday to stop the sale of pieces of the border wall.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Jones, NewsNation

item.title: Senator says RFK Jr told him he agrees with Trump on abortion, will have light touch regulating farmers
item.description: GOP Alabama Sen. Tommy Tuberville said Robert F. Kennedy Jr., President-elect Donald Trump's pick for Health and Human Services secretary, told him he stands behind Trump's stance on abortion 100% and will not go overboard when it comes to regulating the agriculture industry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico tightens bonds with immigrant community in US
item.description: The government of Mexico is strengthening bonds with its citizens in the United States, pledging to assist them at a time immigrant communities could be coming under increased scrutiny here.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court shuts down Texas doctors suing Biden admin over transgender policy
item.description: A federal court of appeals unanimously blocked three Texas doctors from suing President Biden's administration over its transgender policies on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massie drops colorful analogy opposing foreign aid, mocks Speaker Johnson with AI-generated image
item.description: Thomas Massie likened U.S. foreign aid to an individual watering their neighbor's yard while their own house burns, and illustrated his point with an AI image of Speaker Mike Johnson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congress agrees to fully fund Baltimore Key Bridge rebuild in plan to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional leaders struck a deal to avert a government shutdown that includes a provision to cover the cost of rebuilding the Francis Scott Key Bridge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court will hear arguments over the law that could ban TikTok in the US if it's not sold
item.description: The Supreme Court will hear arguments next month over the constitutionality of the federal law that could ban TikTok in the U.S. if its Chinese parent company doesn’t sell it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China has expanded its nuclear force and strengthened ties to Russia, the Pentagon says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — China is expanding its nuclear force, has increased military pressure against Taiwan and has strengthened its ties with Russia over the past year, according to a Pentagon report Wednesday that details actions accelerating key areas of conflict with the United States.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden honors the memory of his first wife and baby daughter who died in a 1972 car crash
item.description: President Joe Biden is remembering his first wife and baby daughter 52 years after they were killed in a car crash in Delaware.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New national poll reveals approval ratings for Biden, Trump amidst transition
item.description: With one month left in office, President Biden's approval rating is hitting a new low in a new national poll, and President-elect Donald Trump's numbers are on the rise
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Electoral College formally votes to confirm Trump's win
item.description: President-elect Trump has clinched the necessary number of Electoral College votes to become the next occupant of the White House, teeing up the official certification of his victory before Congress next month.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHS rolls out new rules for skilled foreign workers on H-1B visas
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security on Tuesday announced new rules for hiring foreign workers that was met with praise for allowing employers to fill jobs quicker and maintaining strict security oversight
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: GOP report recommends Liz Cheney be criminally investigated over Jan. 6 work
item.description: It’s a claim that if pursued would likely face significant roadblocks but that nonetheless comes as Trump has suggested members of the Jan. 6 panel should “go to jail” for their work.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats pick Gerry Connolly for Oversight post, rejecting Ocasio-Cortez bid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Democrats on Tuesday picked 74-year-old Rep. Gerry Connolly to lead the party next year atop an influential congressional committee, pushing aside growing calls for generational change in leadership ahead of a second term for Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress strikes deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional leaders have struck a bipartisan deal to keep the government funded beyond a looming Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada's 6 Electoral College votes certified for President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Nevada Republican Party Chairman Michael McDonald officially certified the state's six Electoral College votes for President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday during a gathering on the UNR campus in Reno.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Horsford vows to defend Nevada families as priority for government cuts grows
item.description: Nevada Democratic Rep. Steven Horsford is doing more than just serving as a watchdog on Republican initiatives to cut government bureaucracy -- he's participating in the process.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A couple hundred North Korean troops killed, wounded in battles with Ukrainian forces
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A couple hundred North Korean troops fighting alongside Russian forces against Ukraine have been killed or wounded during battle in the Kursk border region, a senior military official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jill Biden says she has closed the book on teaching at Northern Virginia Community College
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jill Biden has closed the book on her teaching career at a Virginia community college. The first lady, who has spent the past 40 years teaching in classrooms, announced Monday that she had taught her final class at Northern Virginia Community College last week.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Andrew Lipow, president of consulting firm Lipow Oil Associates, said a 25 percent tariff amounts to a surcharge of about 40 cents per gallon.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Kamala Harris tells students to 'stay in the fight' after the election
item.description: The vice president's remarks to students in the Maryland Corps service year program were her most extensive since her concession speech. She did not disclose her own plans for after her term ends, but she spoke about “our shared commitment to the work ahead.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Freedom Caucus reelects Andy Harris as chair
item.description: Rep. Andy Harris (R-Md.) will chair the House Freedom Caucus through the end of 2025, members of the group decided Monday night, cementing his leadership of the hard-line conservative caucus.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats divided over Biden administration response to reported drone sightings
item.description: The Biden administration’s response to mounting public concern over alleged drone sightings in the Northeast is dividing Democrats on Capitol Hill, where some lawmakers are defending the executive assurances that there’s no threat to public safety and others are bashing the various agencies for not
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Connolly defeats Ocasio-Cortez to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) on Tuesday defeated Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) to lead Democrats on the Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress, putting the feisty liberal veteran in one of the most powerful spots to confront President-elect Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal agents, hazmat sent to home of Ohio Secretary of State
item.description: Multiple agencies were sent to Ohio Secretary of State Frank Larose's residence for a reported "security incident.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota-backed bill to make bald eagles the national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: The bald eagle could soon become the national bird of the United States after a bill backed by Minnesota legislators passed the U.S. House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: In disputed Shakopee-area legislative race, voters share what was on uncounted ballots
item.description: While 20 ballots believed to have been discarded before being counted won’t be recovered, some voters who cast those missing ballots shared what was on them. They appear to show Rep. Brad Tabke’s narrow lead would stand up.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Senate advances annual defense policy bill, teeing up final vote in coming days
item.description: The Senate on Monday cleared a key procedural hurdle toward passing the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA), teeing up a final vote in the coming days before Congress wraps up its work for the year.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Bill to officially make bald eagle national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: A bill to officially designate the bald eagle as the national bird will head to President Biden’s desk after passage in the House on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: March for Our Lives co-founder David Hogg launches DNC vice chair bid
item.description: Hogg, 24, cited Democrats’ losing ground with young voters in November as a motivating factor to run for the leadership position within the party.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok asks the Supreme Court for an emergency order to block a U.S. ban unless it’s sold
item.description: A similar plea was filed by content creators who rely on the platform for income and some of TikTok’s more than 170 million users in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Journalists anticipate a renewed hostility toward their work under the incoming Trump administration
item.description: For the press heading into a second Trump administration, there's a balancing act between being prepared and being fearful.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation
